summary: EXP-270 wholesale-replaces §4.1 (data/sections.json part4 <details id="p4-41-0">) with refreshed dashboard framing. Title updated from '28-Domain / 47.69σ Dashboard' to '28-Scored / 39-Domain / 61.46σ Dashboard'. Numerical refresh against 2026-04-28T01:19:56Z status_history.json snapshot: σ 47.69→61.46, R² 0.0298→0.0187, r 0.1727→0.1367, p 4.21e-3→3.42e-3, n 520→887, total_domains 28→39 (28 scored + 11 newly-exposed prospective_pending). Adds a 'Vindication note' subsection showing the σ growth matches the Fisher √n mechanical prediction within 1.4% (predicted 62.30 vs observed 61.46) — the count-without-physics critique that the prior section made was a discriminating prediction that landed at the next snapshot. Audit table itself (28 rows) is byte-identical; only the framing prose and per-paragraph numerical values change. After integration: run `tests/run.sh`, then `node build.js`, then close ISS-1649. Also worth a quick cross-page grep for stale '47.69' in docs/index.html, data/wins.json, etc. — that may surface ISS-followups for other sections that referenced this number (low expected, but cheap to check).
decider_action_hint: Replace the entire `<details id="p4-41-0">…</details>` block in data/sections.json's `part4.html` field with the EXP-270 replacement_html. Boundaries are exact and unique. Do not attempt substring patches — the change touches dozens of numbers and several paragraphs and would be error-prone as patch list.
cross_page_followups: After integration, search docs/index.html, data/wins.json (all WIN entries, especially those that mention Live Power dashboard / Fisher / 47.69), and data/sections.json other parts for '47.69' or 'cross-domain sigma' literals. Any stale references are minor follow-up issues. Initial check shows §4.1 is the canonical home of these numbers; cross-references are likely few or zero.
newsworthy: Dome's headline 'evidence' σ rose 29% in ten days with no new physics — fit to data simultaneously got worse. Mechanical prediction landed within 1.4%.
